steps.title: Identify the conflict between prediction and result
steps.content: The theory proposes that a habitat's initial species diversity strongly affects its eventual diversity. Mercer's research did not support this: pools starting with diverse zooplankton and pools starting with a single species showed little to no difference in species diversity by the end of the study.

steps.title: Match the researchers' assessment
steps.content:
C directly expresses what the researchers would conclude: the theory appears logical (“may seem plausible”) but the data did not support it (“not supported by the research team's findings”).
  • A overstates the theory's correctness despite the failed prediction
  • B ignores the evidence entirely
  • D misinterprets refutation as a conditional limitation


That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
